Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Prefix sums at gigabytes per second with ARM NEON 44 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Understanding the Go Runtime: The Scheduler 113 upvotes, 15 comments
  3. The Met releases high-def 3D scans of 140 famous art objects 291 upvotes, 55 comments
  4. IMG_0416 (2024) 97 upvotes, 18 comments
  5. Executing programs inside transformers with exponentially faster inference 110 upvotes, 22 comments
  6. Vite 8.0 Is Out 301 upvotes, 80 comments
  7. Bubble Sorted Amen Break 339 upvotes, 102 comments
  8. Malus – Clean Room as a Service 1277 upvotes, 464 comments
  9. Shall I implement it? No 1282 upvotes, 472 comments
  10. Willingness to look stupid 349 upvotes, 117 comments
  11. “This is not the computer for you” 471 upvotes, 184 comments
  12. Celebrating Interesting Flickr Technologies 38 upvotes, 9 comments
  13. Enhancing gut-brain communication reversed cognitive decline in aging mice 311 upvotes, 129 comments
  14. Worldwide Sidewalk Joy: Adding whimsy to neighborhoods 18 upvotes, 3 comments
  15. Document poisoning in RAG systems: How attackers corrupt AI's sources 120 upvotes, 47 comments
  16. Ceno, browse the web without internet access 22 upvotes, 5 comments
  17. Innocent woman jailed after being misidentified using AI facial recognition 619 upvotes, 319 comments
  18. Bringing Chrome to ARM64 Linux Devices 107 upvotes, 47 comments
  19. Bucketsquatting is (finally) dead 71 upvotes, 30 comments
  20. Never snooze a future 16 upvotes, 4 comments
  21. Runners who churn butter on their runs 104 upvotes, 62 comments
  22. An old photo of a large BBS (2022) 189 upvotes, 131 comments
  23. Big data on the cheapest MacBook 356 upvotes, 280 comments
  24. ATMs didn’t kill bank teller jobs, but the iPhone did 420 upvotes, 445 comments
  25. How people woke up before alarm clocks 55 upvotes, 47 comments
  26. Okmain: How to pick an OK main colour of an image 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. US private credit defaults hit record 9.2% in 2025, Fitch says 367 upvotes, 416 comments
  28. Can you instruct a robot to make a PBJ sandwich? 31 upvotes, 33 comments
  29. Specimen Gallery – CC0 transparent specimen PNGs organized by taxonomy 8 upvotes, 5 comments
  30. Grief and the AI split 154 upvotes, 235 comments

Last generated 6:30pm, 13 March 2026. Source here.
